channel.title: Comments for page "SCP Wiki Usertools"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "SCP Wiki Usertools"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: just wanted to voice my stratospheric appreciation for Rating-B-Gone, thank you.

item.title: Replace Stylish with Stylus
item.content:encoded:
It's been known that Stylish has done some shady things, so I'm recommending the replacement of the open-source fork Stylus

  • Chrome
  • Firefox
  • Opera

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another Vivaldi user? I don't believe it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would like to add that this extension also works with Vivaldi.

item.title: Broken Link
item.content:encoded: The link to the night mode example image leads to a "The file does not exist" error. I'd really love to see what it looks like so that I can decide whether or not to install all the things required to do so, and I believe others would, as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I reckon it be SCPer.
Link: https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/scpper/cpebggobaenfndpoddkdindknnpbjdfc

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There seems to be a lot of requests for a usertool that adds the name of the SCP onto the page when reading; while I know we don't do that for immersion reasons, there's still people that memorize the titles better than the numbers.

I seem to recall that there is a usertool that does that, but it doesn't seem to be on this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should the information on the Usertools page be updated now that there seems to be a Chrome extension available?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The author karma script's reference page seems like it's not being updated regularly- Not sure if that's a (Crayne/Crayne's computer)-being-busy thing or a bot-not-doing-what-it's-supposed-to thing, but I figured I'd mention it.

If it's the former, perhaps someone with the time and bandwidth could adopt it? I personally find it helpful to have up-to-date numbers: it's a good way to catch when someone's posted something new or a new person is posting a lot of stuff that's sticking around.

item.title: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: There should be a usertool that changes the color of nicknames (after the links) based on the SCP's object class. Green should mean Safe, yellow should mean Euclid, red should mean Keter, and any other classes, as well as "[ACCESS DENIED]," should be black.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My pictures of me wearing nothing but a small doggo are gone. You LIED to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The screenshot for night mode is borked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Chrome won't run the usertools as extensions unless they are to be found on the chrome web store.
That's at least what it tells me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems like every userscript working on Chrome also works on MS Edge.

item.title: Re: StaffID down again
item.content:encoded: Wasn't first thing Monday, but shit's been fixed.

item.title: Re: StaffID down again
item.content:encoded: Will fix it first thing Monday, and yeah, that's definitely it. Thanks for the cue.

item.title: StaffID down again
item.content:encoded: StaffID seems to be broken again. I think that's likely because the lookup page on 05 changed.

item.title: Real Votes update
item.content:encoded: Noting that I updated the Real Votes userscript to no longer list whether or not the author upvoted, and to count that vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Posting to note that the HIRTOY? userscript now shows you what you've already read on the Series pages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Works fine for me on all pages in a thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Confirming it works again now.

It only seems to work on the first page of a thread, though. Is that just me, or is it a limitation of the addon/wikidot?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, Athenodora's name change triggered it. Should be fixed now; I'll try to make it more robust.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff ID appears to be broken.

Of course, it could just be my version of Firefox again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're right, it does. I noticed and it's on my to-do list. I might be able to get round to it this week. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Real Ratings module looks odd with the Heritage Collection rating module on Firefox.

item.title: Re: HIRTOY? problems tackled
item.content:encoded:
Sorry to be a bother again so soon, Crayne, but I've been experiencing some new problems with HIRTOY?

Basically, any page requiring HIRTOY? (in other words, any SCP article) will not load the HIRTOY? module properly: it will either take a very long time for the HIRTOY? module to load, or it simply will not load at all. In some instances, after +30 seconds, loading will stop, but there will be 2 or 3 HIRTOY? modules present on the page.

I should note that this appears to prevent the loading of all other greasemonkey usertools, such as the jumpbox. I've tested each tool separately, and the loading problems stopped as soon as I disabled HIRTOY?, so I'm pretty confident that's the problem.

EDIT: I'm also 99% sure it's causing problems with Firefox.

item.title: HIRTOY? problems tackled
item.content:encoded: The HIRTOY? problems as reported by Hercules Rockefeller should be solved now. Please let me know if you're still experiencing difficulties.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the bug report. I'll get on it asap. :)

Having done some digging, it seems that at some point code has gone missing from the backend. I can't find it anywhere, including in backups, so this is fucking weird. What this means is that HIRTOY? will not work until I recreate the missing code. Hang in there…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HIRTOY? (Have I Read This One Yet?) is no longer working for me. It worked well for several months (I use Firefox) but now displays a yellow question mark instead of a green checkmark or a red X on every page, regardless of whether I've read that page or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author Karma is not currently working for me. Everyone's author karma shows up as 0 and totally blank. Not sure what all info is needed, but Firefox, Greasemonkey & Windows 7.

EDIT: And after two days of not working, it now works.

item.title: Re: PM Shortcut Bug
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I noticed that too, but thanks for bringing it to my attention. I need to look at that. :)

item.title: PM Shortcut Bug
item.content:encoded:
I noticed that when I went to the new MTF 2016 Contest Page, in the section with the list of teams & their members, there were 2 PM shortcuts next to the last member of each team. The first shortcut was their actual PM shortcut, but the second shortcut was a shortcut for the first person in the team (AKA, the team captain).

Screenshot

I use Google Chrome, TamperMonkey, & Windows 10.

item.title: Re: SCP 'Blackboxer'
item.content:encoded:
Thanks.

EDIT: If you find any bugs, just PM me.
EDIT2: Forgot error-checking; will fix that as soon as possible.

item.title: Re: SCP 'Blackboxer'
item.content:encoded: I'll take your work out for a spin first and if I don't see any snags, I'll happily put it up for you.

item.title: Re: SCP 'Blackboxer'
item.content:encoded:
…so I can put it up on the main Usertools page? Not sure it'll work for Macs since I don't own one (though it might work with Mono).

There are actually two programs: the original non Metro-style, and the one that's up for download.

item.title: Re: SCP 'Blackboxer'
item.content:encoded:
…or a Mac…

Okay, there was mead involved last night…

item.title: Re: SCP 'Blackboxer'
item.content:encoded: I use a laptop. It's just more convenient that way.

item.title: Re: SCP 'Blackboxer'
item.content:encoded: It's fine, but I have to ask: why not just use ALT+219?

item.title: SCP 'Blackboxer'
item.content:encoded:
Not entirely sure if this is the correct place to post this, but I've created a program that prints out full-block characters (and among other things, like names, dates, SCP designations) so I won't have to copy-paste it from other articles (or use the character map).

Download
(It's an .exe; not sure if that's allowed)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We have plans to do that, but it's not a priority right now. If we do get the userscripts working on Chrome, we will announce it here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you guys get Real Rating Info and HIRTOY? to work on Chrome?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most (all?) of these work in Firefox for Android, FYI.

my £5000 monthly donation

I am going to pretend that I know for sure that you are joking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The pages don't specifically state that they won't work with Chrome mobile (Android, at any rate).

Any chance Android functionality can be added? If not, what is my £5000 monthly donation being spent on?

item.title: Night Mode update
item.content:encoded: Night Mode userstyle has been updated with various minor fixes. If you're using it, you should go install the new version. If you're not using it, you may want to try and see if you like it.

item.title: New userscript added
item.content:encoded: Added the HIRTOY? (Have I Read This One Yet?) script, that keeps track of which SCP articles you've read.

item.title: Moar night mode bugz
item.content:encoded: Okay, so I'm on Win10 using Google Chrome. When scrolling over text in the quote/interview/transcript box thingies(idk what they're called srry) the mouse becomes practically invisible. This was previously a problem with making posts, except it was brought up in chat and quickly fixed by anqxyr. I haven't found the problem elsewhere, and I believe it only to be a problem with matching colors, as it was with the post issue. If you need anymore info or anything, I'm ready to supply, and thanks in advance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you edit the 'Night Mode' script and add 'scpsandbox2.wikidot.com' in the affected domains, it works for it as well. Surprisingly, the background and all other things exclusive to the sandbox remain completely unaffected (As far as I've seen so far).

Interesting.

The usertools as a whole are great, although Night Mode is definitely my favorite. My eyes were dying before this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that the Real Rating Info userscript has been updated to also check for an author upvote. Won't break if you're using an older version though. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
im not sure weather I love Night Mode yet or not, but the change is nice….
Good Job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My eyeballs would like to thank the person(s) who made Night Mode and introduced me to Stylish for the reduction in the number of searing-white webpages they have to deal with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the only way for it to work is if Crayne manages to get it on the Web Store?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't. Chrome Devs don't want you using scripts that haven't been vetted through their Web Store - apparently there were a rash of bad-faith extensions that culminated in the crackdown. I know Crayne was looking into getting them put on the store, but I don't know how far he got.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I downloaded all the plugins, but when I came on the computer tommorow, it said chrome disabled them.

Anybody know how to enable extensions even when chrome won't let you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uploaded a new version of the PM Shortcut userscript that has a lot less impact on performance. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hah, didn't take it that way, don't worry. :)

I'm looking into getting the scripts into the Chrome web store. No news on that one yet though.

Also, just uploaded a new version of the real votes userscript that makes it so you can instantly see current vote data, not just the data when you loaded the page. This means that if you click it before you voted on a page, you'll see something different after you vote. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it turns out that Chrome (at least the one I have, v40.something) won't allow me to load extensions that aren't on the Chrome Web Store. It seems to be a hardcoded lockout the Chrome devs put in to "protect" the users.

Edit Note: The original text came off kinda snotty. This isn't Crayne's day job, and I don't want to give the impression that I'm ungrateful for the work he does here in his theoretically-free time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added the "Real Rating Info" userscript, as requested by Mistbourne. :)

Edit: many, many thanks to anqxyr for sharing his arcane knowledge of Wikidot fuckery and how to hack the shit out of it ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Updated the PM Shortcut userscript to run a bit more smoothly.
